description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline, mutagen.cli

Follow-up to mutagen-wrd.24. The behaviour facades (ExUnit, ExUnit.Server, CaptureIO, Compiler, Cover) replaced the 14 test-seam apply/3 sites in Baseline, CoverageRunner, MutationRunner, and MutationLoop. The Mix task's pipeline dispatch table (lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex) still uses {mod, fun} tuples with 15 apply/3 call sites for the 11 phase keys (:cli, :scope, :tests, :ast_cache, :coverage, :enumerator, :baseline, :mutation, :reporter_ok, :reporter_error, :io + legacy :reporter, :pipeline).

The deferred replacement: define a per-phase behaviour (or a single MutagenEx.Pipeline.Collaborator behaviour-per-key family) and convert each dispatch slot to a plain module atom. Tests using {__MODULE__, :__phase_X__} (heavily in json_reporter_test.exs's capture_dispatch/1) will need adjustment to swap module atoms.

Why deferred from .24:
- The .24 deliverable explicitly named only ExUnit/Compiler/Cover facades.
- The phase dispatch has 11+ different arities; the facade pattern is not a uniform fit.
- json_reporter_test.exs's heavy {__MODULE__, :__phase_X__} pattern requires coordinated test rewrites that go beyond "tests swap modules without changes".

Closes the remaining half of F9 (HIGH, Idioms H2) once .24 lands.

## Allowed touches
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex
- test/mutagen_ex/json_reporter_test.exs (capture_dispatch rewrite)
- test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs (if affected)
- lib/mutagen_ex/pipeline/ (new behaviours)

## Verification
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- mix test green
- No apply/3 call sites remain in lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex except for explicit back-compat fallback paths.

Filed during mutagen-wrd.24 (post-review hardening epic).

Files touched (production):
- lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex — dispatch table now holds module atoms for the 11 phase keys; 15 apply/3 sites → 0 in the new code paths; 2 apply/3 sites remain in run_legacy/2, the explicit back-compat fallback the ticket's Verification block carves out.

- lib/mutagen_ex/pipeline/default_io.ex (NEW, default :io impl — lifted out of Mix.Tasks.Mutagen so the dispatch slot can be a plain module atom)
- lib/mutagen_ex/cli.ex (@behaviour CliFacade + @impl on parse/1)
- lib/mutagen_ex/scope_resolver.ex (@behaviour ScopeFacade + @impl on resolve/2)

- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_enumerator.ex (@behaviour EnumeratorFacade + @impl on enumerate/4)
- lib/mutagen_ex/baseline.ex (@behaviour BaselineFacade + @impl on run/1)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (@behaviour MutationFacade + @impl on run/1)
- lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex (@behaviour ReporterOk + ReporterError + @impl on emit_report/1, emit_error/2)

Files touched (test):
- test/mutagen_ex/json_reporter_test.exs — capture_dispatch/1 rewritten. The pre-.33 {__MODULE__, :__phase_X__} trampolines were replaced with 8 per-phase stub modules (PhaseStubs.Scope, .Tests, .AstCache, .Coverage, .Enumerator, .Baseline, .Mutation, .Io). Each stub implements the facade and reads its body from Process.get({:phase_fun, :name}) so the closure-injection power is preserved.
- test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs — parse-time --json safety test migrated. Defined PhaseStubs.Scope + PhaseStubs.Io modules; the {__MODULE__, :__phase_*__} dispatch entries became plain module atoms.
- test/mutagen_ex/mix_task_phase_scope_test.exs — also affected (the phase_scope O(n)/order tests use the dispatch seam). 4 collaborator helper functions migrated to per-test modules: CliPassthrough, PhaseScopeCollaborator, PhaseScopeIo, PhaseTestsAbort.
- test/mutagen_ex/json_canonicalisation_test.exs — also affected (s10c/s10d/s10e symlink tests). 2 helper functions migrated to PhaseStubs.Scope + PhaseStubs.Io modules.
- test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs — also affected (`run_pipeline!/1` dispatch). 3 collaborator helper functions migrated to per-test modules: CaptureIoCollaborator, BaselineCollaborator, CoverageCollaborator. `:tests` and `:mutation` slots now use the production modules directly.

Tests added: none new — the existing test surface fully exercises the dispatch refactor. All tests using the old `{module, function}` shape were rewritten to the new module-atom shape, asserting the Mix task dispatches correctly via module atoms.

Verification stubs flipped: none. No spec-verification stub names the dispatch shape; the contract is internal (.spec/AGENTS.md: "facade structure is internal — specs describe observable behavior"). mix compile --warnings-as-errors green continues to be the practical Dialyzer surrogate.

Audit-expanded scope: N/A (iteration 1). The ticket's Allowed touches enumerate `test/mutagen_ex/json_reporter_test.exs (capture_dispatch rewrite)` and `test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs (if affected)`. Three other test files were broken by the same dispatch shape change and migrated under the same intent: test/mutagen_ex/mix_task_phase_scope_test.exs, test/mutagen_ex/json_canonicalisation_test.exs, test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs. The ticket's narrative explicitly anticipates this — "Tests using {__MODULE__, :__phase_X__} ... will need adjustment to swap module atoms" — and the Allowed-touches list reads as illustrative ("if affected"), not exhaustive. Calling it out so the auditor can confirm or rebuke.

Production modules outside the literal Allowed list also got minimal annotations (single-line `@behaviour` + per-callback `@impl`): MutagenEx.CLI, ScopeResolver, TestSelector, AstCache, CoverageRunner, MutationEnumerator, Baseline, MutationRunner, JsonReporter. These annotations are mechanically required for the new behaviours to bind to their production defaults under --warnings-as-errors; without them, every callsite of `module.callback(args)` against a default that didn't declare @behaviour would compile but lose the Dialyzer-surrogate gate. Same justification pattern as .24 (which expanded the production modules' annotation surface for the same reason).

Deferred must requirements: none. Both ticket gates met:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors green (exit 0).
- mix test green: 506 tests, 0 failures, 15 excluded.
- mix test --include e2e_slow green: 519 tests, 0 failures, 2 excluded.
- mix test --include e2e_slow --include spike: 521 tests, 0 failures (the 4-tests/2-failures group reported in the spike output is from LaneFixture.*Test red-suite fixtures that the e2e tests intentionally run to assert mutation kills — overall pipeline tests pass).
- `apply/3` count in lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex: 2 (both inside run_legacy/2, the explicit back-compat fallback). 0 in the new state-machine paths.

Summary: Defined 11 per-phase behaviours under MutagenEx.Pipeline.*Facade + 1 default IO impl (MutagenEx.Pipeline.DefaultIo, lifted from Mix.Tasks.Mutagen's default_io/3). The Mix task's dispatch table now carries plain module atoms for the 11 phase keys and dispatches via `mod.callback(args)` — 15 production apply/3 sites became 0 (the two remaining apply/3 calls are inside run_legacy/2, the S1 back-compat fallback). 9 production modules gained `@behaviour` + `@impl` annotations so callback drift fails --warnings-as-errors. The pre-.33 test fixture pattern of `{__MODULE__, :__phase_X__}` tuples backed by `Process.put`-keyed closures was migrated to per-test PhaseStubs.* modules across 5 test files; the closure-injection power is preserved (the stub bodies still read from Process.get({:phase_fun, :name}) so individual tests can plant per-phase bodies without minting a new module per case). The legacy `:reporter`/`:pipeline` two-key dispatch shape stays as `{module, function}` tuples and routes through run_legacy/2 unchanged, satisfying the back-compat carve-out in the ticket's Verification block.

VERIFIED

Scope check vs Allowed touches: in-scope are lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex, test/mutagen_ex/json_reporter_test.exs, test/mutagen_ex/cli_test.exs, lib/mutagen_ex/pipeline/ (new behaviours). Outside the literal Allowed list: (a) the 9 production modules touched to add @behaviour + @impl annotations — mechanically required for --warnings-as-errors binding; same pattern as mutagen-wrd.24; diffs are pure annotation + formatter reflow, no semantic change; (b) 3 test files (mix_task_phase_scope_test.exs, json_canonicalisation_test.exs, end_to_end_test.exs) — Allowed list says "if affected"; ticket narrative anticipates these ("Tests using {__MODULE__, :__phase_X__}... will need adjustment"); implementer flagged both expansions in their IMPLEMENTED comment. Audit-expanded scope accepted. No Out-of-Scope drift (the ticket has no explicit Out-of-Scope block; the F9 closure boundary is honored — only the Mix dispatch surface + its test seams changed).

Project verification gates (executed literally from stage worktree):
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors — exit 0 (clean).
- mix test — 506 tests, 0 failures (15 excluded), exit 0 — matches implementer's claimed 506 green.

Verification block contract:
- "No apply/3 call sites remain in lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex except for explicit back-compat fallback paths." — confirmed via `grep -nE 'apply\\(' lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex`: exactly 2 apply/3 sites, both inside `run_legacy/2` (lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:285 dispatching :pipeline; :291 dispatching :reporter). Matches implementer's claim. The 11 new-path phase keys (:cli, :scope, :tests, :ast_cache, :coverage, :enumerator, :baseline, :mutation, :reporter_ok, :reporter_error, :io) all dispatch via `mod.callback(args)` direct calls — verified by reading run_pipeline/2 → phase_*/N + emit_success/emit_abort.

Source-claim trace:
- 11 new behaviour modules under lib/mutagen_ex/pipeline/ — CONFIRMED (ast_cache_facade, baseline_facade, cli_facade, coverage_facade, enumerator_facade, io_facade, mutation_facade, reporter_error_facade, reporter_ok_facade, scope_facade, tests_facade); plus default_io.ex as the IoFacade production impl.
- Dispatch table converted to module atoms — CONFIRMED (lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:1021-1040 `default_dispatch/0`: 11 keys map to module atoms; :reporter/:pipeline stay as {module, function} tuples per back-compat carve-out).
- 9 production modules declare @behaviour + @impl — CONFIRMED (grep returned 9 distinct production modules with @behaviour MutagenEx.Pipeline.*; json_reporter declares 2 behaviours; default_io adds an 11th @behaviour pairing for IoFacade).
- json_reporter_test.exs's capture_dispatch/1 rewritten with PhaseStubs.* modules — CONFIRMED (json_reporter_test.exs:799-878 defines 8 PhaseStubs.* modules: Scope, Tests, AstCache, Coverage, Enumerator, Baseline, Mutation, Io).
- 8 PhaseStubs.* test modules preserving closure-injection via Process.get/put — CONFIRMED. Each stub's @impl callback body is `apply(Process.get({:phase_fun, :name}), args)`; tests plant per-phase bodies via `Process.put({:phase_fun, k}, fun)` (capture_dispatch/1:884). The test ergonomics changed (modules instead of {__MODULE__, :__phase_X__} tuples) but the expressive surface is identical — per-test closure injection still works without minting a new module per case. Zero `__phase_` tokens remain across all 26 test files (`grep -c '__phase_' test/mutagen_ex/*.exs` → all 0). Equivalent power confirmed.

No fixes attempted, no ticket closure. Reporting only.

title: Replace Mix.Tasks.Mutagen dispatch table {mod, fun} with module atoms
